description: Allowlist for batch_verify.py — functions that structurally cannot pass with random-integer register seeds, plus harness-unmappable (oracle crashes in zero-fill Unicorn), non-terminating (instruction-budget), and no-delinked-ref cases. The last three are infra/harness limits, not lift bugs; re-enable a function by removing it here once a --state-snapshot or delinked-ref makes it runnable. Wire in with --allowlist tools/equivalence/batch_verify_allowlist.json --skip-allowlisted.

reasons: oracle crash: @esi=deflate_state* — random seed dereferences state struct fields immediately
oracle crash: @edx=state (deflate_state*) — random seed dereferences state struct fields immediately
oracle crash: @eax=deflate_state* — random seed dereferences state+0x1448 (heap_len) on first access
oracle crash: @eax=tree (ct_data*) — random seed used as struct pointer, crashes on first field access
oracle crash: @ecx=deflate_state* — random seed dereferences state struct fields immediately
oracle crash: @eax=deflate_state* — random seed dereferences state struct fields immediately
oracle crash: @eax=deflate_state* — random seed dereferences state struct fields, returns state in eax
oracle crash: @eax=state (deflate_state*) threaded through FUN_00117130 — random seed dereferences state+0x16ac
oracle crash: @eax=bl_count (short[16]*) — random seed used as array pointer, crashes on first element access

oracle-unmappable: ORACLE-CRASH (UC_ERR_FETCH_UNMAPPED eip=0x43ff3d) on some seeds; leaked into divergence bucket. Harness limit, not a lift bug.
non-terminating within instruction budget (INSN-LIMIT oracle=1000001); leaked into divergence bucket. Harness limit, not a lift bug.

harness artifact, not a lift bug (adjudicated 2026-07-08 vs live KotH snapshot). Oracle assert-string reloc s_king_globals.hill_point_count_!=_0026d5d4 is un-emulatable (logged as trace-end); candidate stub-string is emulatable (logged as display_assert). Calls 0..80 byte-identical across all seeds. 0x456c38==0 in both because find_netgame_flags (2nd-level callee in b1180) is stubbed->0 and --real-callees does not recurse. Live gameplay reads hill_point_count=4, proving the real path returns 1. Faithful port; VC71/source is the evidence.
